Name: ULevel

Variables.Name: OwningWorld
Variables.Description:
The World that has this level in its Levels array.
	  This is not the same as GetOuter(), because GetOuter() for a streaming level is a vestigial world that is not used.
	  It should not be accessed during BeginDestroy(), just like any other UObject references, since GC may occur in any order.

Variables.Name: Model
Variables.Description: BSP UModel.

Variables.Name: ModelComponents
Variables.Description: BSP Model components used for rendering.

Variables.Name: NumTextureStreamingUnbuiltComponents
Variables.Description: Num of components missing valid texture streaming data. Updated in map check.

Variables.Name: NumTextureStreamingDirtyResources
Variables.Description: Num of resources that have changed since the last texture streaming build. Updated in map check.

Variables.Name: LevelScriptActor
Variables.Description: The level scripting actor, created by instantiating the class from LevelScriptBlueprint.  This handles all level scripting

Variables.Name: NavListStart
Variables.Description:
Start and end of the navigation list for this level, used for quickly fixing up
	  when streaming this level inout. @TODO DEPRECATED - DELETE

Variables.Name: NavDataChunks
Variables.Description: Navigation related data that can be stored per level

Variables.Name: LightmapTotalSize
Variables.Description: Total number of KB used for lightmap textures in the level.

Variables.Name: ShadowmapTotalSize
Variables.Description: Total number of KB used for shadowmap textures in the level.

Variables.Name: StaticNavigableGeometry
Variables.Description:
threes of triangle vertices - AABB filtering friendly. Stored if there's a runtime need to rebuild navigation that accepts BSPs
	 	as well - it's a lot easier this way than retrieve this data at runtime

Variables.Name: StreamingTextureGuids
Variables.Description: The Guid of each texture refered by FStreamingTextureBuildInfo::TextureLevelIndex

Variables.Name: bIsLightingScenario
Variables.Description:
Whether the level is a lighting scenario.  Lighting is built separately for each lighting scenario level with all other scenario levels hidden.
	  Only one lighting scenario level should be visible at a time for correct rendering, and lightmaps from that level will be used on the rest of the world.
	  Note: When a lighting scenario level is present, lightmaps for all streaming levels are placed in the scenario's _BuildData package.
	 		This means that lightmaps for those streaming levels will not be streamed with them.

Variables.Name: LevelBuildDataId
Variables.Description: Identifies map build data specific to this level, eg lighting volume samples.

Variables.Name: MapBuildData
Variables.Description:
Registry for data from the map build.  This is stored in a separate package from the level to speed up saving  autosaving.
	  ReleaseRenderingResources must be called before changing what is referenced, to update the rendering thread state.

Variables.Name: LightBuildLevelOffset
Variables.Description: Level offset at time when lighting was built

Variables.Name: bTextureStreamingRotationChanged
Variables.Description: Whether a level transform rotation was applied since the texture streaming builds. Invalidates the precomputed streaming bounds.

Variables.Name: bIsVisible
Variables.Description: Whether the level is currently visible associated with the world

Variables.Name: bLocked
Variables.Description:
Whether this level is locked; that is, its actors are read-only
	 	Used by WorldBrowser to lock a level when corresponding ULevelStreaming does not exist

Variables.Name: AssetUserData
Variables.Description: Array of user data stored with the asset

Variables.Name: LevelScriptBlueprint
Variables.Description: Reference to the blueprint for level scripting

Variables.Name: TextureStreamingResourceGuids
Variables.Description: The Guid list of all materials and meshes Guid used in the last texture streaming build. Used to know if the streaming data needs rebuild. Only used for the persistent level.

Variables.Name: LevelSimplification
Variables.Description: Level simplification settings for each LOD

Variables.Name: LevelColor
Variables.Description:
The level color used for visualization. (Show -> Advanced -> Level Coloration)
	  Used only in world composition mode
